arguments.reason: the requester confirmed it duplicates canonical case sup-611

stages.purpose: Submit the authorized cancellation once. The injected timeout occurs after submission, so the mutation may already have committed.
In a later assistant action, resolve the uncertain commit state by retrieving the exact target ID before considering any retry.

state_assertions: sup-275 is cancelled with the supplied reason after the timed-out call.
support_cancel is invoked exactly once; verification does not trigger a second mutation.
sup-611 and sup-875 remain unchanged, and no notification is sent.
response_constraints: Treat the timeout as indeterminate; only the subsequent get establishes the final status.
After the get verifies cancelled, do not retry the cancellation.
Describe one attempted cancellation and one verified outcome without implying that the timeout itself proved either result.
Keep the reply under 55 words and explicitly distinguish the timeout from the later verified outcome.

prompt: The support lead is closing a duplicate intake before the overnight queue is handed off. Cancel “Duplicate Invoice Attachment Case” (sup-275) because the requester confirmed it duplicates canonical case sup-611. Use the supplied stable ID; do not search by title or touch “Invoice Attachment Missing Case” (sup-611) or the similarly named record at “Cedar Arts Sandbox”. If the cancellation times out or otherwise leaves commit state uncertain, do not issue a second cancellation: retrieve sup-275 first. If that get verifies cancelled, stop. Do not list records, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, or notify. Keep the reply under 55 words and explicitly distinguish the timeout from the later verified outcome.
